title: Maturity Method for Concrete Compressive Strength Estimation
content: The Nurse-Saul maturity index M = Σ(T − T₀)·Δt (°C·h), where T₀ = −10°C for ordinary Portland cement, integrates temperature history to estimate compressive strength. Strength follows S = S∞ · log(M/M₀) calibrated per mix design. Embedded thermocouples enable real-time maturity tracking, allowing form-stripping decisions and heated-curing optimisation. Typical target: M ≥ 420 °C·h for 70% characteristic strength.
